title: Weigh the directories
prompt: Disk is filling up and you need to know WHERE. du -s * summarizes the size of each item in the current directory (du -sh makes it human-readable). One of the three top-level directories here is dramatically heavier than the others. Write its name (just the name, e.g. media) into answer.txt.

details.restate: Measure the three top-level directories and write the name of the heaviest one into answer.txt — just the directory name on one line.

io.because: -s summarizes each argument instead of listing every file inside

terms.meaning: disk usage — how much space files and directories actually take

help.plain: Ask for one summary line per directory and compare three numbers.
help.analogy: Weighing three moving boxes instead of cataloguing every item inside them.
help.nudge: du can report one total per directory instead of a thousand per-file lines. Which flag?
